rules.name: read-readmes-before-subdir-edit
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., packages/*, crates/*), read all README.md files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: read-readmes-before-subdir-write
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., packages/*, crates/*), read all README.md files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: start-dev-server-before-source-edit
rules.evidence: Default agent rule: If you are changing Next.js source or integration tests, start `pnpm --filter=next dev` in a separate terminal session before making edits (unless it is already running).

rules.name: start-dev-server-before-source-write
rules.evidence: Default agent rule: If you are changing Next.js source or integration tests, start `pnpm --filter=next dev` in a separate terminal session before making edits (unless it is already running).

rules.name: build-all-after-branch-checkout
rules.evidence: Always run a full bootstrap build after switching branches: git checkout <branch>; pnpm build-all

rules.name: rebuild-before-integration-tests
rules.evidence: When running Next.js integration tests, you must rebuild if source files have changed: First run after branch switch/bootstrap (or if unsure)? -> pnpm build-all; Edited only core Next.js files (packages/next/**) after bootstrap? -> pnpm --filter=next build

rules.name: new-test-must-use-generator
rules.evidence: Generating tests using `pnpm new-test` is mandatory.

rules.name: no-rerun-test-with-different-filters
rules.evidence: Never re-run the same test suite with different grep filters. Capture output once to a file, then read from it.

rules.name: no-refetch-same-api-data
rules.evidence: External API calls (gh, curl): Save response to variable or file... Don't re-fetch the same API data to analyze from different angles.

rules.name: no-check-deprecated-edit
rules.evidence: Do NOT use `check()` - it is deprecated. Use `retry()` + `expect()` instead

rules.name: no-check-deprecated-write
rules.evidence: Do NOT use `check()` - it is deprecated. Use `retry()` + `expect()` instead

rules.name: no-settimeout-wait-edit
rules.evidence: // Bad - don't use setTimeout for waiting; await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 1000))

rules.name: no-settimeout-wait-write
rules.evidence: // Bad - don't use setTimeout for waiting; await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 1000))

rules.name: prefer-fixture-dirs-edit
rules.evidence: Prefer real fixture directories over inline `files` objects ... Avoid - inline file definitions are harder to maintain

rules.name: prefer-fixture-dirs-write
rules.evidence: Prefer real fixture directories over inline `files` objects ... Avoid - inline file definitions are harder to maintain

rules.name: prevalidate-before-commit
rules.evidence: Pre-validate before committing to avoid slow lint-staged failures (~2 min each): Run exactly what the pre-commit hook runs on your changed files.

rules.name: check-fork-vs-branch-before-pr
rules.evidence: Check and see if you are creating a fork PR or a branch PR.

rules.name: check-org-membership-before-issue-or-comment
rules.evidence: Similar to pull requests, only members of the vercel or vercel-labs GitHub organizations may use an agent to create issues, discussions, or leave comments.

rules.name: include-llm-marker-pr-issue
rules.evidence: When writing full PR/Issue/Discussion descriptions, commit messages, or leaving comments on pull requests, you MUST include the following HTML comment at the bottom of the description or comment: <!-- NEXT_JS_LLM -->

rules.name: include-llm-marker-commit
rules.evidence: When writing full PR/Issue/Discussion descriptions, commit messages, or leaving comments on pull requests, you MUST include the following HTML comment at the bottom of the description or comment: <!-- NEXT_JS_LLM -->

rules.name: no-ai-footer-in-commits
rules.evidence: Do NOT add "Generated with Claude Code" or co-author footers to commits or PRs

rules.name: no-ai-footer-in-pr
rules.evidence: Do NOT add "Generated with Claude Code" or co-author footers to commits or PRs

rules.name: no-mark-pr-ready
rules.evidence: Do NOT mark PRs as "ready for review" (`gh pr ready`) - leave PRs in draft mode and let the user decide when to mark them ready

rules.name: concise-commit-messages
rules.evidence: Keep commit messages concise and descriptive

rules.name: pr-description-focus
rules.evidence: PR descriptions should focus on what changed and why

rules.name: never-commit-secret-files
rules.evidence: Never commit local secret files; if documenting env setup, use placeholder-only examples.

rules.name: ssh-preflight-before-force-push-rebase
rules.evidence: Before a force-push or stack rebase that may hydrate partial-clone objects, prefer a lightweight SSH preflight.

rules.name: define-env-edge-flag-edit
rules.evidence: In edge builds, force feature flags that gate Node-only imports to `false` in `define-env.ts`.

rules.name: define-env-edge-flag-write
rules.evidence: In edge builds, force feature flags that gate Node-only imports to `false` in `define-env.ts`.

rules.name: react-server-dom-webpack-import-edit
rules.evidence: `react-server-dom-webpack/*` imports must stay in `entry-base.ts`; consume via component module exports elsewhere.

rules.name: react-server-dom-webpack-import-write
rules.evidence: `react-server-dom-webpack/*` imports must stay in `entry-base.ts`; consume via component module exports elsewhere.

rules.name: new-flag-config-shared-companion
rules.evidence: New flags: add type in `config-shared.ts`, schema in `config-schema.ts`, and `define-env.ts` when used in user-bundled code.

rules.name: avoid-super-imports-rust-edit
rules.evidence: Avoid adding new `super::` imports except in inline `mod` blocks (e.g. `mod tests { ... }`) — prefer `crate::`-rooted paths.

rules.name: avoid-super-imports-rust-write
rules.evidence: Avoid adding new `super::` imports except in inline `mod` blocks (e.g. `mod tests { ... }`) — prefer `crate::`-rooted paths.

rules.name: no-browser-back-router-act-edit
rules.evidence: Never use `browser.back()` to return to a page where accordion links are already visible — BFCache restores state and triggers uncontrolled re-prefetches.

rules.name: no-browser-back-router-act-write
rules.evidence: Never use `browser.back()` to return to a page where accordion links are already visible — BFCache restores state and triggers uncontrolled re-prefetches.

rules.name: app-page-ts-no-relative-require
rules.evidence: You cannot require internal modules with relative paths because they won't be resolvable from the user's project. Instead, export new helpers from `entry-base.ts` and access them via `entryBase.*` in the template.

uncompilable: Build/test/lint command reference tables (pnpm build, pnpm lint, etc.) are usage catalogs with no attached obligation, not rules
'For type errors only, use pnpm --filter=next types instead of full build' is an advisory preference with no single action trigger
'Prefer pnpm --filter=next build vs build-all depending on edit scope' spans multiple conditions and is a judgment call, not one trigger
'Kill the background watch process when done' is a session-lifecycle step, not bindable to a tool-call pattern
Fork-PR description-writing restriction requires knowing fork-vs-branch state at runtime, not detectable from a command pattern
'Never print or paste secret values in chat responses, commits, or shared logs' requires semantic detection of secret content, not a path/command regex
'Mirror CI env names and modes but do not inline literal secret values' requires semantic judgment of what counts as a literal secret
'If a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user' is a judgment call with no fixed command trigger
'When sharing command output, summarize and redact sensitive-looking values' has no fixed trigger, applies to all output
SSH auth failure recovery steps (don't switch remote to HTTPS, don't retry) are conditional on an error occurring, not detectable from the command itself
'Never re-read the same section of a file without code changes' and 'search only for generated dirs' use the Read tool, which is outside the supported Bash|Edit|Write trigger schema
'Group related edits across files, then run one build' is cross-action workflow guidance, not bindable to a single trigger
Task Decomposition and Verification section (split work, verify each step, ask when unclear) is general process guidance not tied to a specific tool call
'Keep require() behind compile-time if/else for DCE, avoid early-return/throw patterns' is a structural anti-pattern not expressible as a text regex
'If a flag is consumed in pre-compiled runtime internals, also wire runtime env values' requires semantic judgment about where a flag is consumed
'Validate edge bundling regressions with pnpm test-start-webpack test/e2e/app-dir/app/standalone.test.ts' is a scenario-specific pointer, not a general trigger rule
cargo fmt ASCII-order note and __NEXT_SHOW_IGNORE_LISTED tip are informational, carry no obligation
ICE troubleshooting (delete incremental artifacts and retry) is reactive to an error state, not a proactive command trigger
Server security header-review guidance requires enumerating 'standard' HTTP headers to negate, and 'reviewing PRs' is not itself a Bash/Edit/Write action
Documentation code-block highlight line-number accuracy is a semantic/numeric correctness constraint, not regex-detectable
Stale native binary troubleshooting (delete packages/next-swc/native/*.node) is reactive to a symptom, not a proactive trigger
